does: This paper systematically evaluates how large language models from major organizations handle value trade-offs through over 300,000 test queries designed to stress-test their specifications. The researchers probe situations where different stated values conflict or where ambiguous instructions create contradictory responses, revealing that Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and xAI models exhibit distinct and sometimes inconsistent patterns in how they prioritize different values. The work identifies thousands of concrete cases where models contradict their own documented specifications or show inconsistency across similar scenarios, exposing gaps between claimed values and actual behavior.
connects: This work extends recent efforts in AI safety and alignment research that seek to understand and characterize model behavior beyond standard benchmarks. It builds on prior work examining model consistency, value alignment verification, and specification testing that emerged as concerns about scaling model capabilities outpaced our ability to verify their adherence to stated principles. The systematic stress-testing approach draws from adversarial testing methodologies and specification-checking frameworks used in software engineering, adapting them to the novel challenge of validating alignment properties across different model implementations. This research responds to growing recognition that informal or incomplete specifications create alignment risks as models become more capable.
enables: This paper enables more rigorous evaluation frameworks for assessing whether models actually implement their stated values in practice, supporting future work in alignment verification and model governance. It establishes baseline findings about inconsistencies that could drive improvements in how AI companies document and enforce their model specifications. The catalog of contradictions and ambiguities provides concrete test cases for developing better specification languages and automated checking procedures. The comparative analysis across different organizations suggests opportunities for industry-wide standards for value documentation and testing. This work also enables better oversight mechanisms by revealing where current specifications are insufficient, potentially informing regulatory approaches to AI evaluation and accountability.
